data.narrative:
Defense Unicorns, founded by Rob Slaughter, Jeff McCoy, and Andrew Greene, is a Series B-stage defense and security technology company currently valued at $1.0B post-money with $171M raised to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of 10 total comparables: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers, with primary reliance on 5 same-sector, same-stage private companies.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression method applies a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $1.9B. The Peer-Median method benchmarks against 5 Defense & Security Series B companies—Vannevar Labs ($1.5B), Dream Security ($1.5B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Heven AeroTech ($1.0B)—yielding a median valuation of $1.4B with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.2B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The gap between methods reflects uncertainty around the applicability of the stage-progression multiplier relative to current peer valuations. Key uncertainties include execution risk relative to peer progress metrics and sector-specific capital availability dynamics in the current defense technology funding environment.
